plan_metadata.name: Feature Verify Feature Flag Filtering In Wizard Options

steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the New Project dropdown of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, highlighted by a red crosshair, to start creating a declarative agent for Microsoft 365 Copilot.

Click the "Add an Action" option in the dropdown menu under "Add an action to your declarative agent" in the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the text input field labeled "Select how to add an action" within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code, opening the dropdown menu for adding a new action.
@assertion "Start with an Office Add-in Action" exists.
Press the Esc key to close the "Create an Action" dropdown menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface within Visual Studio Code, dismissing the action selection overlay.
Open the settings panel in Visual Studio Code by pressing the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+, while in the "Build a Declarative Agent" workspace for Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit.

Type the text 'M365AgentsToolkit.enableDeclarativeAgentOfficeAddIn' into the settings search input field within the Settings panel of Visual Studio Code, under the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click the checkbox to enable "Enable Declarative Agent In Office Add In" for the M365AgentsToolkit extension in the Visual Studio Code settings panel.
Press the Escape key to close the Settings modal editor in Visual Studio Code, specifically while in the "Extensions > M365 Agents Toolkit" settings section.
Click the "Create a Agent/App" blue button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar within Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (170, 301).
Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the New Project dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, selecting the agent creation type for Microsoft 365 Copilot.

Click the "Add an Action" option in the dropdown menu under "Add an action to your declarative agent" within the "Create Declarative Agent" interface of Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion "Start with an Office Add-in Action" doesn't exist.
